title: Pregnancy Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
in your uterus to provide oxygen and nutrients to the fetus. The placenta starts making hCG after a fertilized egg attaches to the inside wall of your uterus. Levels then continue to rise for ten weeks, almost doubling every three days.
hCG helps your body prepare for pregnancy by:
Telling your body to produce progesterone, which thickens the lining of your uterus. 
Telling your body to produce estrogen, which prepares your body for labor and helps your fetus develop. 
Stopping your menstrual periods . 
There are two types of pregnancy tests:
Urine tests , which can be done at your health care provider's office or with an at-home test Â kit. These tests are 97-99% accurate when done a week or two after you've missed your period and can give you the results quickly. If you do an at-home test, follow the instructions carefully. 
Blood tests , which are always done in your provider's office or a lab. These tests can find very small amounts of hCG and show whether you're pregnant before you've missed your period. However, they aren't commonly used to check for pregnancy. That's because urine tests are less expensive, very accurate, and provide quicker results than blood tests, which may take hours to more than a day. 
Other names: human chorionic gonadotropin test, HCG test, qualitative hCG blood test, quantitative hCG blood test, Beta-hCG urine test, total chorionic gonadotropin,
